title: chamaesciadium spinosum
intro: Chamaesciadium spinosum, also known as the rough chervil or wild rue, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Mediterranean. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and carminative effects. The health benefits of Chamaesciadium spinosum include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in various cultures. Chamaesciadium spinosum is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and its essential oil is used in aromatherapy and topical applications.
